metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A high elves city where High-Elven Prism Arboretum of Dawn Bridges, Lecture Hall Beacon, Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Luminthalas should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Luminthalas organizes power through Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court, public response through Lecture Hall Beacon, civic memory through High-Elven Prism Arboretum of Dawn Bridges, and daily life through S1 University / Lyceum Civic Hall, S2 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, S3 Legislative Quarter Hall, S4 Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, S5 Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Moonstone, Granite, Cedar, Gold Leaf, Pale Marble Stone, Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Moonlight Refraction, and exact anchors including Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court, Lecture Hall Beacon, High-Elven Prism Arboretum of Dawn Bridges, Alchemy Lab, Star Observatory, Temple Platform, University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook, Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A high elves civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: High-Elven Prism Arboretum of Dawn Bridges, Lecture Hall Beacon, Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court, and section anchors such as University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, Legislative Quarter Hall, Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall.

canonLocks: Luminthalas uses city code LUM.
The Wonder is High-Elven Prism Arboretum of Dawn Bridges.
The Beacon is Lecture Hall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.

The source-backed section spine is: S1 University / Lyceum Civic Hall, S2 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, S3 Legislative Quarter Hall, S4 Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, S5 Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Luminthalas as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

streets.appearance: Streets should show Luminthalas's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 University / Lyceum Civic Hall, S2 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, S3 Legislative Quarter Hall, S4 Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, S5 Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall. streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.

streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.

streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
